title: Understanding Quantifiers in Logic
description: A visual explanation of universal (∀) and existential (∃) quantifiers, showing their scope, application to variables, and resulting proposition truth values.
relationships: ∀x P(x) is T only if P(x) is T for ALL x in the domain
∃x P(x) is T if P(x) is T for AT LEAST ONE x in the domain
Quantifiers bind variables to the propositional function
